item.title: pg_plan_advice: Plan Stability and User Planner Control for PostgreSQL?
item.description: I'm proposing a very ambitious patch set for PostgreSQL 19. Only time will tell whether it ends up in the release, but I can't resist using ...

item.title: eLife Fallout
item.description: When a prestigious journal scrapped accept-reject decisions on submitted papers, some scientists rebelled, and the editor-in-chief was fired.

item.title: Stable Problems
item.description: We treat early agreement on a problem as stability. But is it?

item.title: Self-evolving software is the future | errgent
item.description: Software should be self-healing, self-improving and self-evolving. It's the end of the human code review.

item.title: Produced by Human
item.description: I outsourced my own role in a simulation I was building — and it worked better without me. That made me wonder what's left for humans when intelligence is no longer our edge.
item.author: (Marius Anderie)

item.title: How good is Claude, really?
item.description: An exploration of Claude Code in the macOS app dev world. Mostly me, watching Claude write macOS apps from scratch, learn a programming language it never heard of, reverse engineer a window manager and make me question the future and life in general.

item.title: Rodney L. - I Lost $150 in 20 Minutes Market-Making on Kalshi. Here's Every Bug That Did It.
item.description: Published 2025-3-5

item.title: BM25
item.description: There is a particular kind of respect reserved in engineering for the algorithm that outlives its era. BM25 is one of them. BM25 was born out of information retrieval research in the 1970s and 1980s, polished over decades, and eventually adopted as the default ranking function in Elasticsearch, Solr, and Lucene.

item.title: How my application programmer instincts failed when debugging assembler
item.description: I've had a smidge of extra time with my recent unemployment, so to stay sharp and learn a few new things I followed Seiya Nuta's guide to building an Operating System in 1,000 Lines.

item.title: The Training Data Paradox: AI Replacing Engineers Who Built It
item.description: AI learned from decades of human engineering. Now the industry is eliminating the developers who created that knowledge. What happens when the source runs dry?

item.title: Inside the Modern Search Engine: The Complete Pipeline — How It Ranks Results & Learns from…
item.description: Modern web search isn’t magic; it’s a massive engineering feat. Crawlers scour billions of pages, hybrid indexes organise that data, query…
item.author: (https://blog.ivan.digital)

item.title: AI Is Quietly Reversing 20 Years of Progress Toward an Open Internet
item.description: The End of the Crawlable Web : For most of the modern internet, there was an implicit contract between websites and search engines. Websites made their content accessible. Search engines indexed it. And in return, search engines sent users back to the original site.

item.title: Future shock – Ceejbot's notes
item.description: Software development in the age of gen AI

item.title: I Wail, For My Tailscale Fails-- How My Packets Got Dropped Beyond the Pale
item.description: How My Packets Got Dropped Beyond the Pale

item.title: A GitHub Issue Title Compromised 4,000 Developer Machines
item.description: A prompt injection in a GitHub issue triggered a chain reaction that ended with 4,000 developers getting OpenClaw installed without consent. The attack composes well-understood vulnerabilities into something new: one AI tool bootstrapping another.
item.author: (grith team)

item.title: The Government Uses Targeted Advertising to Track Your Location. Here's What We Need to Do.
item.description: The online advertising industry has built a massive surveillance machine, and the government can co-opt it to spy on us.

item.title: Wikimedia Status
item.description: Welcome to Wikimedia's home for real-time and historical data on system performance.

item.title: The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying
item.description: Questioning the frame of inevitability in use of AI

item.title: GitHub - googleworkspace/cli: Google Workspace CLI — one command-line tool for Drive, Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, Docs, Chat, Admin, and more. Dynamically built from Google Discovery Service. Includes AI agent skills.
item.description: Google Workspace CLI — one command-line tool for Drive, Gmail, Calendar, Sheets, Docs, Chat, Admin, and more. Dynamically built from Google Discovery Service. Includes AI agent skills. - googlework...

item.title: Gabriele Svelto (@gabrielesvelto@mas.to)
item.description: A few years ago I designed a way to detect bit-flips in Firefox crash reports and last year we deployed an actual memory tester that runs on user machines after the browser crashes. Today I was looking at the data that comes out of these tests and now I'm 100% positive that the heuristic is sound and a lot of the crashes we see are from users with bad memory or similarly flaky hardware. Here's a few numbers to give you an idea of how large the problem is. 🧵 1/5
